about.title: Engineer DNA
about.subtitle: How I think, build, and grow as an engineer.
bio_paragraphs: I am an Electronics and Communication Engineering undergraduate at LNMIIT Jaipur who enjoys building complete engineering systems. My interests span software engineering, embedded systems, artificial intelligence, and electronics, with a focus on understanding how these disciplines work together rather than in isolation.

narrative.positioning: Harshil Jain is a systems-focused engineer who builds complete products across software, embedded systems, and artificial intelligence. Rather than specializing in a single layer of the stack, he enjoys understanding how hardware, software, and intelligence work together to solve real engineering problems—from first principles to production.
narrative.professional_summary: Electronics & Communication Engineering undergraduate at LNMIIT Jaipur with a multidisciplinary focus spanning software engineering, embedded systems, and artificial intelligence. Passionate about designing production-ready systems through strong engineering fundamentals, practical implementation, and continuous learning.
narrative.career_mission: Build intelligent systems that seamlessly integrate hardware, software, and artificial intelligence while continuously learning, documenting, and contributing to meaningful engineering solutions.

philosophy.title: Understand the Whole System
philosophy.body: I believe engineering problems rarely belong to a single discipline. A hardware bug can surface as a software issue, and an AI system is only as reliable as the software and hardware beneath it. Rather than treating these layers separately, I enjoy understanding the complete system before building or optimizing individual components.

differentiators: Builds complete systems across hardware, software, and AI.
Every project is backed by code, documentation, demonstrations, or measurable outcomes.
Treats documentation with the same importance as implementation.
Focused on understanding and owning the entire engineering lifecycle—from design to deployment.
narrative.short_bio: Electronics & Communication Engineering undergraduate building production-ready software, embedded systems, and AI solutions with a systems-first engineering mindset.
long_bio: Harshil Jain is a B.Tech Electronics & Communication Engineering student at LNMIIT, Jaipur — fifth semester, 7.79 CGPA, 95.83 percentile at JEE Main. He works across three areas: embedded hardware (PCB design, FPGA, microcontrollers, robotics), full-stack development (React, Next.js, Node.js, MongoDB), and applied AI (computer vision, AI agents).

That range comes with results: a Frontend Developer internship at AIMargdarshak, Creative Lead for ASME EFx 2026, and hackathon placements at Smart India Hackathon, Samsung Solve for Tomorrow, Flipkart GRID 8.0, and Cognee Hangover AI. Certified in PCB Design (Altium, LNMIIT) and Embedded Systems (LNMIIT).
Long term, he wants to own products fully — the hardware and the software — rather than one piece of either.

footer.tagline: An engineer's operating system. Built on first principles. Designed for real-world systems.

recruiter.title: Why Hire Me?
recruiter.subtitle: Every project, achievement, certification, and experience on this portfolio is backed by code, documentation, demonstrations, or measurable results—not marketing.
recruiter.pitch:
I enjoy solving engineering problems that span more than one discipline. My work combines software engineering, embedded systems, and artificial intelligence to build complete, production-oriented solutions rather than isolated components.

Alongside my academic journey at LNMIIT Jaipur, I have gained practical experience through internships, engineering projects, hackathons, and technical certifications. Every claim on this portfolio is supported by working code, documentation, demonstrations, or measurable outcomes.

If you're looking for someone who learns quickly, takes ownership, and enjoys building complete engineering systems from first principles to production, I'd love to contribute to your team.

developer.mode_summary: I build software the same way I build engineering systems—starting from first principles, prioritizing maintainability, documenting decisions, and treating architecture as seriously as implementation.
